requirements_met: memory_supersede MCP tool implementation with validation
Register tool via TASK-MCP-001 extension point
Reject malformed keys with validation message
Reject empty predecessor list with clear error
Handle store timeout via degradation wrapper
Unit tests covering all validation cases and error handling
Integration tests verifying search exclusion and forward supersession
validation_results.test_command: pytest tests/integration/test_mcp_supersede.py tests/unit/test_mcp_projects_resource.py tests/unit/test_mcp_search.py tests/unit/test_mcp_supersede.py tests/unit/test_mcp_write.py -v --tb=short
validation_results.test_output_summary: 36 passed, 5 deselected in 0.90s. All unit tests for validation, error handling, and timeouts passed. Integration tests verified supersession marking, search exclusion, and forward supersession behavior.
validation_results.code_quality: All files pass ruff lint checks. Implementation follows established patterns for MCP tools (register function, @tool_safe decorator, natural key validation).
edge_cases_covered: Malformed key validation (both predecessor and successor)

criteria_verification.notes: File src/fleet_memory/mcp/tools/supersede.py exists with register() function that registers memory_supersede_tool via FastMCP server using the established extension point pattern.

Integration test test_superseded_memory_drops_out_of_default_search confirms: (1) predecessor marked with superseded_by field, (2) excluded from default search (include_superseded=False), (3) appears when include_superseded=True. Test passes in independent run.
Unit test test_single_predecessor_accepted verifies single predecessor processing. Integration tests also use single predecessor successfully. Both pass.
Unit test test_empty_predecessor_list_rejected verifies empty list triggers ValueError with exact message 'At least one predecessor is required'. Implementation validates at lines 65-66. Test passes.

Unit test test_malformed_predecessor_rejected verifies malformed keys rejected with 'not a valid memory key' message. Helper function _validate_natural_key enforces type:project:identifier format. Test passes.

Unit tests test_self_supersession_rejected and test_self_supersession_in_list_rejected verify self-supersession detection with message 'a memory cannot supersede itself'. Implementation checks at lines 84-85. Tests pass.

Unit test test_forward_supersession_accepted and integration test test_forward_supersession_takes_effect_after_write verify forward supersession accepted and applied when predecessor eventually written. Tests pass.

Unit test test_store_timeout_degrades_gracefully verifies TimeoutError caught by @tool_safe decorator and converted to infrastructure error with 'memory store' message. Server stays running. Test passes.
Ruff check on tests/integration/test_mcp_supersede.py returned 'All checks passed!'. All modified files pass linting.

rationale: All 9 acceptance criteria verified through 36 passing independent tests (8 unit tests covering validation/error handling/timeout, 4 integration tests covering search behavior and forward supersession). Quality gates passed. BDD oracle experienced technical error (feature file not found) but is not a required gate and does not indicate implementation defect. Honesty discrepancies are minor report generation artifacts (including orchestrator-managed and prior-turn files) not critical fabrications. Implementation complete and correct.
